name: evaluation-monitoring
summary: Continuous evaluation of agent output against metrics — success rate, latency, cost, quality. Feedback loop informs prompt and pattern refinement.
when_to_use: Production or semi-production agents where regression is costly. Also for tuning prompts, skills, agents before shipping.

layers.skill: skills/context-budgeting covers session analytics. skills/plugin-development has test and eval scaffolding.
layers.hook: PostToolUse / Stop hooks can log metrics to .claude/metrics.jsonl.
layers.agent: No dedicated agent; part of the dev loop — use plugin-architect during authoring.

cost: ~5% overhead during normal runs. Evaluation runs are explicit and batched.
anti_patterns: Only evaluating after deploy — regressions ship.
Metrics without context — raw numbers without rubric miss quality regressions.
Not logging the rubric version alongside scores — scores become non-comparable over time.
